nativeRange.habitat: Intertidal and shallow subtidal zones; commonly found in estuaries, lagoons, and sheltered coastal areas on soft substrate or attached to shells and rubble
nativeRange.biotope: Shallow marine and estuarine; intertidal to shallow subtidal, often in nutrient-enriched coastal waters

decorPreferences: open water column or refugium tumbling
rockwork anchor point for holdfast attachment

goodWith: herbivorous tang as live food source

imageCandidates.notes: Green Ogo morph — primary morph, dark green bushy fronds
Red Spike Sphere morph — bright red/burgundy with pointed fronds
Mocha Spike Sphere morph — golden brown coloration due to xanthin pigments
Mocha Ogo morph — dark reddish-brown hues

summary: Fast-growing reef macroalgae sold under four color-morph trade names (Green Ogo, Red Spike Sphere, Mocha Spike Sphere, Mocha Ogo). Color is determined by light intensity and nutrient levels rather than genetics — all four are the same species.
careNotes:
FOUR COLOR MORPHS, ONE SPECIES: Green Ogo, Red Spike Sphere, Mocha Spike Sphere, and Mocha Ogo are all Gracilaria tikvahiae. Color is determined by light intensity and nutrient stress, not by genetic difference. Higher light and higher nutrients tend to promote green coloration; lower light and lower nutrients tend to produce red, mocha, and brown tones. Aquarists sometimes purchase the same species twice under different trade names without realizing it.

CARE: Generally regarded as one of the most beginner-friendly reef macroalgae. Does well both in a refugium (tumbled gently with moderate flow) and as a display specimen. Accepts a range of saltwater conditions typical of established reef systems. Supplemental iron, iodine, and trace elements may support growth in nutrient-lean systems, but established tanks usually provide sufficient nutrients naturally.

NUTRIENT EXPORT: Consumes nitrates and phosphates effectively. Fast growth rate makes it a useful refugium worker, though growth rate depends on available nutrients and light.

FEEDING: Reef-safe; trimmings are palatable to tangs and other herbivorous fish and can be offered as live food.

ALGAEBARN CARE LEVEL NOTE: AlgaeBarn rates this species as 'moderate' on their product pages, likely reflecting their general rating system for marine macroalgae. The broader reef-keeping hobby widely considers Gracilaria species to be beginner-accessible given their hardiness and tolerance of normal reef parameters.

SIBLING SPECIES IN TRADE: Related Gracilaria species sold separately include Thick Ogo (G. sp., sw-macro-003), Red Ogo (G. parvispora, sw-macro-007), Pom Pom (G. hayi, sw-macro-006), Red Spaghetti (G. pacifica, sw-macro-015), and Monkey Finger (G. salicornia, sw-macro-014). Per Mosaic care guide guidance: as a red or brown alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand — attach to rock via superglue, zip-tie/fishing line, or wedging instead. (Sand-bed acceptance per Mosaic is limited to Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea genera, plus some calcified Dasycladales.)
breedingNotes: Reproduces readily in the aquarium by fragmentation — broken or trimmed pieces can reattach and grow into new colonies. Gracilaria species also undergo alternation of generations (gametophyte-sporophyte life cycle) in natural settings, but hobbyist propagation is primarily through physical fragmentation.

additional.name: AlgaeBarn — Red Spike Sphere
additional.notes: Same species (G. tikvahiae), red color morph. Water parameters confirmed identical: 72-78°F, dKH 8-12, pH 8.1-8.4, SG 1.023-1.025.

additional.name: AlgaeBarn — Mocha Spike Sphere
additional.notes: Same species (G. tikvahiae), mocha color morph. Temperature range listed as 55-80°F on this page; the broader range is likely a generic macroalgae template and less specific than the 72-78°F listed for the primary green morph. Used for size confirmation (~4 inch max diameter).

additional.name: AlgaeBarn — Mocha Ogo
additional.notes: Same species (G. tikvahiae), mocha ogo morph. Temperature range listed as 60-83°F; pH 7.8-8.4. Wider temperature range on mocha pages likely a generic template. Confirmed SG 1.023-1.025 and dKH 8-12 match other morphs.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Gracilaria (genus)
additional.notes: Confirmed family Gracilariaceae, order Gracilariales. Genus distribution described as warm waters worldwide with diversity center in Western Pacific; G. tikvahiae specifically is a well-documented western Atlantic species from scientific literature.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
